Early heatwave grips Surat with temperatures reaching 38°C:Cool mornings breeze & scorching afternoons raise risk of viral infections
- Copy link
Summer has officially knocked in southern parts of Gujarat as temperatures are seeing a massive increase. Surat has recorded a maximum temperature of 37.9 degrees today (March 5), causing citizens to experience scorching heat. With the mercury reaching close to 38 degrees in the early days of March itself, signs of a harsh summer are being seen in the coming days.
According to meteorological department data, the city recorded a minimum temperature of 20.2 degrees. Due to this shift in weather patterns the city's residents are experiencing dual seasons with mild cold in early morning and intense heat in the afternoon.
Humidity levels remained high at 80%
Along with the heat, significant humidity levels are also being observed. The humidity level remained high at around 80%. When temperature raises and humidity levels are also being elevated with normal wind speed, it leads to increased stuffiness and discomfort. With wind speed being almost zero, people sweating profusely had to seek refuge in air-conditioned offices and homes with fans.
Possibility of increase in dehydration and viral infection cases
The sudden change in weather conditions is also showing its impact on health. Due to humidity and intense afternoon heat, there are concerns about an increase in cases of dehydration and viral infections.
Health experts have advised citizens to avoid direct sun exposure and to drink as much water and fluids as possible to maintain proper hydration levels in the body. The Met department has also advised people not to step out if its not necessary.
Temperature likely to rise further
According to the local weather office, atmospheric pressure was recorded at 1014.7 hpa. If westerly winds start blowing in the coming week, the temperature could further increase by another 1 to 2 degrees.
